steps.title: Identify the cubic structure from the graph
steps.content: The graph shows a cubic function with a negative leading coefficient $a$ (curve descends on the right side where $x \to \infty$). There are two turning points: a local maximum in the region near $x = -1$ with a positive $y$-value, and a local minimum near $x = 2$ with $y \approx -12$.

steps.title: Count x-intercepts by tracing from left to right
steps.content:
Trace the curve across the x-axis:
  • Coming from the upper-left ($y \to +\infty$ as $x \to -\infty$), the curve crosses the x-axis around $x \approx -4$ (first intercept)
  • The curve rises to its local maximum above the x-axis, then descends and crosses again around $x \approx 0$ to $x \approx 1$ (second intercept)
  • The curve then reaches its local minimum below the x-axis at $y \approx -12$
  • For all $x > 2$, the curve remains negative and continues descending, never crossing the x-axis again
The total count is 2 distinct x-intercepts where $f(x) = 0$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
